class.name: FmodAudioEffectDistortion
class.brief_description: Distortion and overdrive effect with multiple distortion modes.
class.description: FmodAudioEffectDistortion applies various types of distortion to audio signals, creating effects ranging from subtle overdrive to harsh digital artifacts. It supports multiple distortion modes including hard clipping, soft clipping, tube overdrive, lo-fi reduction, and bitcrushing. Use this effect to add grit, warmth, or aggressive character to audio.

method.name: get_keep_hf_hz
method.description: Returns the high-frequency preservation cutoff in Hertz.

method.name: get_post_gain
method.description: Returns the post-distortion gain in decibels.

method.name: get_pre_gain
method.description: Returns the pre-distortion gain in decibels.

method.name: set_drive
method.description: Sets the distortion drive/amount. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Higher values produce more distortion.

method.name: set_keep_hf_hz
method.description: Sets the high-frequency preservation cutoff in Hertz. Frequencies above this value are preserved and mixed back with the distorted signal.

method.name: set_mode
method.description: Sets the distortion mode. See [enum Mode] for available options.

method.name: set_oversample
method.description: Sets the oversampling factor. Higher values reduce aliasing artifacts.

method.name: set_post_gain
method.description: Sets the post-distortion gain in decibels. Use this to compensate for volume changes caused by distortion.

method.name: set_pre_gain
method.description: Sets the pre-distortion gain in decibels. Higher values drive the distortion harder.

members.member: The distortion drive/amount. Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Higher values produce more distortion.
The high-frequency preservation cutoff in Hz. Frequencies above this value are preserved and mixed back with the distorted signal to maintain clarity.
The distortion mode. See [enum Mode] for available options.
The oversampling factor. Higher values reduce aliasing artifacts at the cost of more CPU usage.
The post-distortion gain in decibels. Use this to compensate for volume changes.
The pre-distortion gain in decibels. Higher values drive the distortion harder.

constants.constant: Hard clipping distortion. Creates harsh, aggressive distortion by sharply limiting the signal.
Arctangent soft clipping. Creates smooth, tube-like distortion with gradual saturation.
Lo-fi reduction. Reduces bit depth and sample rate for a retro digital sound.
Overdrive. Simulates analog tube amplifier distortion with warm, musical saturation.
Wave shaping distortion. Uses a waveshaping function to create harmonic distortion.
Bit crush. Reduces bit depth for aggressive digital degradation artifacts.
